mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle, which typically reduces the cost of capital and boosts corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 consistently show a negative correlation with a falling FEDFUNDS rate over any 3-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.4 percentage points or more over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If, after the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a rapidly rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening to curb demand, which severely impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 5% AND FEDFUNDS rises by 0.75% or more in 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both inflation is high and rates are rising aggressively.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) growth indicates strong consumer demand and confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns, particularly during periods of economic expansion.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS growth and S&P 500 5-day returns turns negative during confirmed expansionary periods, or if the signal's alpha is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) in such regimes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a leading indicator of future economic activity and consumer confidence, suggesting stronger demand across various sectors and thus supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If a sustained increase in HOUST does not precede or coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 65% of occurrences during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signifies robust manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which underpins corporate earnings and indicates a healthy economic environment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show a negative or zero correlation with INDPRO growth over any 4-year rolling window, indicating a decoupling of industrial activity from market performance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth indicates a robust labor market, leading to higher consumer income and spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly increase is greater than 150,000 persons
failure_condition: If, when the trigger is met, the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a restrictive monetary policy stance, which raises borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by 1.0 percentage point or more over a 6-month period
evidence_refs: fact:rate hikes increase cost of capital
failure_condition: If, following the trigger condition, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of triggered periods during a confirmed tightening cycle.
